items.front: What lateral minimum applies within the Gulf of America Low Offshore airspace between flight paths defined by published Grid System waypoints?
What does the oceanic boundary-crossing method require when an aircraft enters airspace with a larger lateral minimum?
items.back: Use the smaller separation at the boundary and keep at least 15 degrees of divergence until the larger minimum is established.

items.front: At and above FL240, how much route-protected airspace may be retained before widening begins?
items.back: Reduce the protected airspace to 10 miles on each side of centerline out to 114.29 miles from the NAVAID, then widen on the specified angle.

items.front: What basic divergence is needed for same-NAVAID track separation?
items.back: For VOR, VORTAC, or TACAN radials, use at least 15 degrees of divergence once one aircraft is clear of the other's protected airspace.
